arts.ttl: Report: Crystal Palace star set to leave the club after eight years
arts.sum: Crystal Palace have decided to part ways with Jeffrey Schlupp, according to reports. TBRFootball have suggested that the Eagles player, whose contract at Selhurst Park expires this summer, is liked by Millwall and Major League Soccer (MLS) clubs, including San Diego FC and Charlotte FC. The Ghanaian midfielder could leave before his contract ends in […] The post Report: Crystal Palace star set to leave the club after eight years appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Mateta and Guéhi strike as Crystal Palace extend Leicester losing run
arts.athr: Peter Lansley at the King Power Stadium
arts.sum: Some of the biggest cheers from Leicester supporters all night came when a flag-waving pitch-invader managed to evade several stewards late on before being brought down. Unfortunately for Leicester, that appears to be their direction of travel. Goals in the second half from Jean‑Philippe Mateta and Marc Guéhi enabled Crystal Palace to equal the club’s best unbeaten away record in the top flight of six successive matches, moving them eight points clear of a relegation zone in which Leicester now appear entrenched. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace punish wasteful Leicester to deepen Ruud van Nistelrooy plight
arts.sum: Jean-Philippe Mateta and Marc Guehi on target as Foxes slip to sixth consecutive Premier League loss

arts.ttl: Leicester ratings v Palace as Jamie Vardy and Jannik Vestergaard punished
arts.sum: City had 21 shots, the most they’ve had in a Premier League fixture this season, but Jamie Vardy missed two golden first-half opportunities, with Boubakary Soumare hitting the bar in the second half. Within 45 seconds, Facundo Buonanotte pinched the ball in the Palace box and set up a chance for Vardy, whose effort was blocked by Marc Guehi.

arts.ttl: Leicester 0-2 Crystal Palace: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Marc Guehi stars for the Eagles
arts.sum: Crystal Palace set a new club record as they extend their unbeaten away run in the Premier League to six matches by snatching a 2-0 victory over dominant Leicester City.

arts.ttl: Leicester lose again thanks to Crystal Palace’s second-half show
arts.sum: Jean-Philippe Mateta and Marc Guehi struck in the second half.

arts.ttl: Source: Ex-Palace star Zaha in talks over MLS move
arts.sum: Former Premier League star Wilfried Zaha is in talks to join MLS franchise Charlotte FC, sources have confirmed to ESPN.

arts.ttl: Chelsea recall Chalobah from Palace loan
arts.sum: The defender returns to Stamford Bridge after four and a half months at Selhurst Park.

arts.ttl: Leicester sign defender Coulibaly in £3m deal
arts.sum: Leicester City sign French full-back Woyo Coulibaly from Parma for a reported fee of £3m.

arts.ttl: Breaking: Ruud van Nistelrooy's first Leicester City transfer confirmed
arts.sum: Ruud van Nistelrooy has sealed his first signing as Leicester City manager with right-back Woyo Coulibaly joining on a four-and-a-half-year contract from Italian side Parma. The deal is a cheap one on account of Coulibaly having just six months remaining on his Parma contract.
